# Sprint Status - Implementation Tracker
name: sprint-status
description: 'Summarize sprint status and surface risks. Use when the user says "check sprint status" or "show sprint status"'
author: "BMad"

# Critical variables from config
config_source: "{project-root}/_bmad/bmm/config.yaml"
user_name: "{config_source}:user_name"
communication_language: "{config_source}:communication_language"
document_output_language: "{config_source}:document_output_language"
implementation_artifacts: "{config_source}:implementation_artifacts"

# Workflow components
installed_path: "{project-root}/_bmad/bmm/workflows/4-implementation/sprint-status"
instructions: "{installed_path}/instructions.md"

# Inputs
sprint_status_file: "{implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml"

# Smart input file references
input_file_patterns:
  sprint_status:
    description: "Sprint status file generated by sprint-planning"
    whole: "{implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml"
    load_strategy: "FULL_LOAD"

file.content:
<task id="_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml" name="Execute Workflow" internal="true">
  <objective>Execute given workflow by loading its configuration, following instructions, and producing output</objective>

  <llm critical="true">
    <mandate>Always read COMPLETE files - NEVER use offset/limit when reading any workflow related files</mandate>
    <mandate>Instructions are MANDATORY - either as file path, steps or embedded list in YAML, XML or markdown</mandate>
    <mandate>Execute ALL steps in instructions IN EXACT ORDER</mandate>
    <mandate>Save to template output file after EVERY "template-output" tag</mandate>
    <mandate>NEVER skip a step - YOU are responsible for every steps execution without fail or excuse</mandate>
  </llm>

  <WORKFLOW-RULES critical="true">
    <rule n="1">Steps execute in exact numerical order (1, 2, 3...)</rule>
    <rule n="2">Optional steps: Ask user unless #yolo mode active</rule>
    <rule n="3">Template-output tags: Save content, discuss with the user the section completed, and NEVER proceed until the users indicates
      to proceed (unless YOLO mode has been activated)</rule>
  </WORKFLOW-RULES>

  <flow>
    <step n="1" title="Load and Initialize Workflow">
      <substep n="1a" title="Load Configuration and Resolve Variables">
        <action>Read workflow.yaml from provided path</action>
        <mandate>Load config_source (REQUIRED for all modules)</mandate>
        <phase n="1">Load external config from config_source path</phase>
        <phase n="2">Resolve all {config_source}: references with values from config</phase>
        <phase n="3">Resolve system variables (date:system-generated) and paths ({project-root}, {installed_path})</phase>
        <phase n="4">Ask user for input of any variables that are still unknown</phase>
      </substep>

      <substep n="1b" title="Load Required Components">
        <mandate>Instructions: Read COMPLETE file from path OR embedded list (REQUIRED)</mandate>
        <check>If template path → Read COMPLETE template file</check>
        <check>If validation path → Note path for later loading when needed</check>
        <check>If template: false → Mark as action-workflow (else template-workflow)</check>
        <note>Data files (csv, json) → Store paths only, load on-demand when instructions reference them</note>
      </substep>

      <substep n="1c" title="Initialize Output" if="template-workflow">
        <action>Resolve default_output_file path with all variables and {{date}}</action>
        <action>Create output directory if doesn't exist</action>
        <action>If template-workflow → Write template to output file with placeholders</action>
        <action>If action-workflow → Skip file creation</action>
      </substep>
    </step>

    <step n="2" title="Process Each Instruction Step in Order">
      <iterate>For each step in instructions:</iterate>

      <substep n="2a" title="Handle Step Attributes">
        <check>If optional="true" and NOT #yolo → Ask user to include</check>
        <check>If if="condition" → Evaluate condition</check>
        <check>If for-each="item" → Repeat step for each item</check>
        <check>If repeat="n" → Repeat step n times</check>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2b" title="Execute Step Content">
        <action>Process step instructions (markdown or XML tags)</action>
        <action>Replace {{variables}} with values (ask user if unknown)</action>
        <execute-tags>
          <tag>action xml tag → Perform the action</tag>
          <tag>check if="condition" xml tag → Conditional block wrapping actions (requires closing </check>)</tag>
          <tag>ask xml tag → Prompt user and WAIT for response</tag>
          <tag>invoke-workflow xml tag → Execute another workflow with given inputs and the workflow.xml runner</tag>
          <tag>invoke-task xml tag → Execute specified task</tag>
          <tag>invoke-protocol name="protocol_name" xml tag → Execute reusable protocol from protocols section</tag>
          <tag>goto step="x" → Jump to specified step</tag>
        </execute-tags>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2c" title="Handle template-output Tags">
        <if tag="template-output">
          <mandate>Generate content for this section</mandate>
          <mandate>Save to file (Write first time, Edit subsequent)</mandate>
          <action>Display generated content</action>
          <ask> [a] Advanced Elicitation, [c] Continue, [p] Party-Mode, [y] YOLO the rest of this document only. WAIT for response. <if
              response="a">
              <action>Start the advanced elicitation workflow {project-root}/_bmad/core/workflows/advanced-elicitation/workflow.xml</action>
            </if>
            <if
              response="c">
              <action>Continue to next step</action>
            </if>
            <if response="p">
              <action>Start the party-mode workflow {project-root}/_bmad/core/workflows/party-mode/workflow.md</action>
            </if>
            <if
              response="y">
              <action>Enter #yolo mode for the rest of the workflow</action>
            </if>
          </ask>
        </if>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2d" title="Step Completion">
        <check>If no special tags and NOT #yolo:</check>
        <ask>Continue to next step? (y/n/edit)</ask>
      </substep>
    </step>

    <step n="3" title="Completion">
      <check>Confirm document saved to output path</check>
      <action>Report workflow completion</action>
    </step>
  </flow>

  <execution-modes>
    <mode name="normal">Full user interaction and confirmation of EVERY step at EVERY template output - NO EXCEPTIONS except yolo MODE</mode>
    <mode name="yolo">Skip all confirmations and elicitation, minimize prompts and try to produce all of the workflow automatically by
      simulating the remaining discussions with an simulated expert user</mode>
  </execution-modes>

  <supported-tags desc="Instructions can use these tags">
    <structural>
      <tag>step n="X" goal="..." - Define step with number and goal</tag>
      <tag>optional="true" - Step can be skipped</tag>
      <tag>if="condition" - Conditional execution</tag>
      <tag>for-each="collection" - Iterate over items</tag>
      <tag>repeat="n" - Repeat n times</tag>
    </structural>
    <execution>
      <tag>action - Required action to perform</tag>
      <tag>action if="condition" - Single conditional action (inline, no closing tag needed)</tag>
      <tag>check if="condition">...</check> - Conditional block wrapping multiple items (closing tag required)</tag>
      <tag>ask - Get user input (ALWAYS wait for response before continuing)</tag>
      <tag>goto - Jump to another step</tag>
      <tag>invoke-workflow - Call another workflow</tag>
      <tag>invoke-task - Call a task</tag>
      <tag>invoke-protocol - Execute a reusable protocol (e.g., discover_inputs)</tag>
    </execution>
    <output>
      <tag>template-output - Save content checkpoint</tag>
      <tag>critical - Cannot be skipped</tag>
      <tag>example - Show example output</tag>
    </output>
  </supported-tags>

  <protocols desc="Reusable workflow protocols that can be invoked via invoke-protocol tag">
    <protocol name="discover_inputs" desc="Smart file discovery and loading based on input_file_patterns">
      <objective>Intelligently load project files (whole or sharded) based on workflow's input_file_patterns configuration</objective>

      <critical>Only execute if workflow.yaml contains input_file_patterns section</critical>

      <flow>
        <step n="1" title="Parse Input File Patterns">
          <action>Read input_file_patterns from loaded workflow.yaml</action>
          <action>For each pattern group (prd, architecture, epics, etc.), note the load_strategy if present</action>
        </step>

        <step n="2" title="Load Files Using Smart Strategies">
          <iterate>For each pattern in input_file_patterns:</iterate>

          <substep n="2a" title="Try Sharded Documents First">
            <check if="sharded pattern exists">
              <action>Determine load_strategy from pattern config (defaults to FULL_LOAD if not specified)</action>

              <strategy name="FULL_LOAD">
                <desc>Load ALL files in sharded directory - used for PRD, Architecture, UX, brownfield docs</desc>
                <action>Use glob pattern to find ALL .md files (e.g., "{output_folder}/*architecture*/*.md")</action>
                <action>Load EVERY matching file completely</action>
                <action>Concatenate content in logical order (index.md first if exists, then alphabetical)</action>
                <action>Store in variable: {pattern_name_content}</action>
              </strategy>

              <strategy name="SELECTIVE_LOAD">
                <desc>Load specific shard using template variable - example: used for epics with {{epic_num}}</desc>
                <action>Check for template variables in sharded_single pattern (e.g., {{epic_num}})</action>
                <action>If variable undefined, ask user for value OR infer from context</action>
                <action>Resolve template to specific file path</action>
                <action>Load that specific file</action>
                <action>Store in variable: {pattern_name_content}</action>
              </strategy>

              <strategy name="INDEX_GUIDED">
                <desc>Load index.md, analyze structure and description of each doc in the index, then intelligently load relevant docs</desc>
                <mandate>DO NOT BE LAZY - use best judgment to load documents that might have relevant information, even if only a 5% chance</mandate>
                <action>Load index.md from sharded directory</action>
                <action>Parse table of contents, links, section headers</action>
                <action>Analyze workflow's purpose and objective</action>
                <action>Identify which linked/referenced documents are likely relevant</action>
                <example>If workflow is about authentication and index shows "Auth Overview", "Payment Setup", "Deployment" → Load auth
                  docs, consider deployment docs, skip payment</example>
                <action>Load all identified relevant documents</action>
                <action>Store combined content in variable: {pattern_name_content}</action>
                <note>When in doubt, LOAD IT - context is valuable, being thorough is better than missing critical info</note>
              </strategy>
              <action>Mark pattern as RESOLVED, skip to next pattern</action>
            </check>
          </substep>

          <substep n="2b" title="Try Whole Document if No Sharded Found">
            <check if="no sharded matches found OR no sharded pattern exists">
              <action>Attempt glob match on 'whole' pattern (e.g., "{output_folder}/*prd*.md")</action>
              <check if="matches found">
                <action>Load ALL matching files completely (no offset/limit)</action>
                <action>Store content in variable: {pattern_name_content} (e.g., {prd_content})</action>
                <action>Mark pattern as RESOLVED, skip to next pattern</action>
              </check>
            </check>
          </substep>

          <substep n="2c" title="Handle Not Found">
            <check if="no matches for sharded OR whole">
              <action>Set {pattern_name_content} to empty string</action>
              <action>Note in session: "No {pattern_name} files found" (not an error, just unavailable, offer use change to provide)</action>
            </check>
          </substep>
        </step>

        <step n="3" title="Report Discovery Results">
          <action>List all loaded content variables with file counts</action>
          <example>
            ✓ Loaded {prd_content} from 5 sharded files: prd/index.md, prd/requirements.md, ...
            ✓ Loaded {architecture_content} from 1 file: Architecture.md
            ✓ Loaded {epics_content} from selective load: epics/epic-3.md
            ○ No ux_design files found
          </example>
          <note>This gives workflow transparency into what context is available</note>
        </step>
      </flow>

    </protocol>
  </protocols>

  <llm final="true">
    <critical-rules>
      • This is the complete workflow execution engine
      • You MUST Follow instructions exactly as written
      • The workflow execution engine is governed by: {project-root}/_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml
      • You MUST have already loaded and processed: {installed_path}/workflow.yaml
      • This workflow uses INTENT-DRIVEN PLANNING - adapt organically to product type and context
      • YOU ARE FACILITATING A CONVERSATION With a user to produce a final document step by step. The whole process is meant to be
      collaborative helping the user flesh out their ideas. Do not rush or optimize and skip any section.
    </critical-rules>
  </llm>
</task>

# Sprint Status - Multi-Mode Service

<critical>The workflow execution engine is governed by: {project-root}/_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml</critical>
<critical>You MUST have already loaded and processed: {project-root}/_bmad/bmm/workflows/4-implementation/sprint-status/workflow.yaml</critical>
<critical>Modes: interactive (default), validate, data</critical>
<critical>⚠️ ABSOLUTELY NO TIME ESTIMATES. Do NOT mention hours, days, weeks, or timelines.</critical>

<workflow>

<step n="0" goal="Determine execution mode">
  <action>Set mode = {{mode}} if provided by caller; otherwise mode = "interactive"</action>

  <check if="mode == data">
    <action>Jump to Step 20</action>
  </check>

  <check if="mode == validate">
    <action>Jump to Step 30</action>
  </check>

  <check if="mode == interactive">
    <action>Continue to Step 1</action>
  </check>
</step>

<step n="1" goal="Locate sprint status file">
  <action>Load {project_context} for project-wide patterns and conventions (if exists)</action>
  <action>Try {sprint_status_file}</action>
  <check if="file not found">
    <output>❌ sprint-status.yaml not found.
Run `/bmad:bmm:workflows:sprint-planning` to generate it, then rerun sprint-status.</output>
    <action>Exit workflow</action>
  </check>
  <action>Continue to Step 2</action>
</step>

<step n="2" goal="Read and parse sprint-status.yaml">
  <action>Read the FULL file: {sprint_status_file}</action>
  <action>Parse fields: generated, last_updated, project, project_key, tracking_system, story_location</action>
  <action>Parse development_status map. Classify keys:</action>
  - Epics: keys starting with "epic-" (and not ending with "-retrospective")
  - Retrospectives: keys ending with "-retrospective"
  - Stories: everything else (e.g., 1-2-login-form)
  <action>Map legacy story status "drafted" → "ready-for-dev"</action>
  <action>Count story statuses: backlog, ready-for-dev, in-progress, review, done</action>
  <action>Map legacy epic status "contexted" → "in-progress"</action>
  <action>Count epic statuses: backlog, in-progress, done</action>
  <action>Count retrospective statuses: optional, done</action>

<action>Validate all statuses against known values:</action>

- Valid story statuses: backlog, ready-for-dev, in-progress, review, done, drafted (legacy)
- Valid epic statuses: backlog, in-progress, done, contexted (legacy)
- Valid retrospective statuses: optional, done

  <check if="any status is unrecognized">
    <output>
⚠️ **Unknown status detected:**
{{#each invalid_entries}}

- `{{key}}`: "{{status}}" (not recognized)
  {{/each}}

**Valid statuses:**

- Stories: backlog, ready-for-dev, in-progress, review, done
- Epics: backlog, in-progress, done
- Retrospectives: optional, done
  </output>
  <ask>How should these be corrected?
  {{#each invalid_entries}}
  {{@index}}. {{key}}: "{{status}}" → [select valid status]
  {{/each}}

Enter corrections (e.g., "1=in-progress, 2=backlog") or "skip" to continue without fixing:</ask>
<check if="user provided corrections">
<action>Update sprint-status.yaml with corrected values</action>
<action>Re-parse the file with corrected statuses</action>
</check>
</check>

<action>Detect risks:</action>

- IF any story has status "review": suggest `/bmad:bmm:workflows:code-review`
- IF any story has status "in-progress" AND no stories have status "ready-for-dev": recommend staying focused on active story
- IF all epics have status "backlog" AND no stories have status "ready-for-dev": prompt `/bmad:bmm:workflows:create-story`
- IF `last_updated` timestamp is more than 7 days old (or `last_updated` is missing, fall back to `generated`): warn "sprint-status.yaml may be stale"
- IF any story key doesn't match an epic pattern (e.g., story "5-1-..." but no "epic-5"): warn "orphaned story detected"
- IF any epic has status in-progress but has no associated stories: warn "in-progress epic has no stories"
  </step>

<step n="3" goal="Select next action recommendation">
  <action>Pick the next recommended workflow using priority:</action>
  <note>When selecting "first" story: sort by epic number, then story number (e.g., 1-1 before 1-2 before 2-1)</note>
  1. If any story status == in-progress → recommend `dev-story` for the first in-progress story
  2. Else if any story status == review → recommend `code-review` for the first review story
  3. Else if any story status == ready-for-dev → recommend `dev-story`
  4. Else if any story status == backlog → recommend `create-story`
  5. Else if any retrospective status == optional → recommend `retrospective`
  6. Else → All implementation items done; congratulate the user - you both did amazing work together!
  <action>Store selected recommendation as: next_story_id, next_workflow_id, next_agent (SM/DEV as appropriate)</action>
</step>

<step n="4" goal="Display summary">
  <output>
## 📊 Sprint Status

- Project: {{project}} ({{project_key}})
- Tracking: {{tracking_system}}
- Status file: {sprint_status_file}

**Stories:** backlog {{count_backlog}}, ready-for-dev {{count_ready}}, in-progress {{count_in_progress}}, review {{count_review}}, done {{count_done}}

**Epics:** backlog {{epic_backlog}}, in-progress {{epic_in_progress}}, done {{epic_done}}

**Next Recommendation:** /bmad:bmm:workflows:{{next_workflow_id}} ({{next_story_id}})

{{#if risks}}
**Risks:**
{{#each risks}}

- {{this}}
  {{/each}}
  {{/if}}

  </output>
  </step>

<step n="5" goal="Offer actions">
  <ask>Pick an option:
1) Run recommended workflow now
2) Show all stories grouped by status
3) Show raw sprint-status.yaml
4) Exit
Choice:</ask>

  <check if="choice == 1">
    <output>Run `/bmad:bmm:workflows:{{next_workflow_id}}`.
If the command targets a story, set `story_key={{next_story_id}}` when prompted.</output>
  </check>

  <check if="choice == 2">
    <output>
### Stories by Status
- In Progress: {{stories_in_progress}}
- Review: {{stories_in_review}}
- Ready for Dev: {{stories_ready_for_dev}}
- Backlog: {{stories_backlog}}
- Done: {{stories_done}}
    </output>
  </check>

  <check if="choice == 3">
    <action>Display the full contents of {sprint_status_file}</action>
  </check>

  <check if="choice == 4">
    <action>Exit workflow</action>
  </check>
</step>

<!-- ========================= -->
<!-- Data mode for other flows -->
<!-- ========================= -->

<step n="20" goal="Data mode output">
  <action>Load and parse {sprint_status_file} same as Step 2</action>
  <action>Compute recommendation same as Step 3</action>
  <template-output>next_workflow_id = {{next_workflow_id}}</template-output>
  <template-output>next_story_id = {{next_story_id}}</template-output>
  <template-output>count_backlog = {{count_backlog}}</template-output>
  <template-output>count_ready = {{count_ready}}</template-output>
  <template-output>count_in_progress = {{count_in_progress}}</template-output>
  <template-output>count_review = {{count_review}}</template-output>
  <template-output>count_done = {{count_done}}</template-output>
  <template-output>epic_backlog = {{epic_backlog}}</template-output>
  <template-output>epic_in_progress = {{epic_in_progress}}</template-output>
  <template-output>epic_done = {{epic_done}}</template-output>
  <template-output>risks = {{risks}}</template-output>
  <action>Return to caller</action>
</step>

<!-- ========================= -->
<!-- Validate mode -->
<!-- ========================= -->

<step n="30" goal="Validate sprint-status file">
  <action>Check that {sprint_status_file} exists</action>
  <check if="missing">
    <template-output>is_valid = false</template-output>
    <template-output>error = "sprint-status.yaml missing"</template-output>
    <template-output>suggestion = "Run sprint-planning to create it"</template-output>
    <action>Return</action>
  </check>

<action>Read and parse {sprint_status_file}</action>

<action>Validate required metadata fields exist: generated, project, project_key, tracking_system, story_location (last_updated is optional for backward compatibility)</action>
<check if="any required field missing">
<template-output>is_valid = false</template-output>
<template-output>error = "Missing required field(s): {{missing_fields}}"</template-output>
<template-output>suggestion = "Re-run sprint-planning or add missing fields manually"</template-output>
<action>Return</action>
</check>

<action>Verify development_status section exists with at least one entry</action>
<check if="development_status missing or empty">
<template-output>is_valid = false</template-output>
<template-output>error = "development_status missing or empty"</template-output>
<template-output>suggestion = "Re-run sprint-planning or repair the file manually"</template-output>
<action>Return</action>
</check>

<action>Validate all status values against known valid statuses:</action>

- Stories: backlog, ready-for-dev, in-progress, review, done (legacy: drafted)
- Epics: backlog, in-progress, done (legacy: contexted)
- Retrospectives: optional, done
  <check if="any invalid status found">
  <template-output>is_valid = false</template-output>
  <template-output>error = "Invalid status values: {{invalid_entries}}"</template-output>
  <template-output>suggestion = "Fix invalid statuses in sprint-status.yaml"</template-output>
  <action>Return</action>
  </check>

<template-output>is_valid = true</template-output>
<template-output>message = "sprint-status.yaml valid: metadata complete, all statuses recognized"</template-output>
</step>

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/juliano.barbosa/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/4.3.1/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents

# Dispatching Parallel Agents

## Overview

When you have multiple unrelated failures (different test files, different subsystems, different bugs), investigating them sequentially wastes time. Each investigation is independent and can happen in parallel.

**Core principle:** Dispatch one agent per independent problem domain. Let them work concurrently.

## When to Use

```dot
digraph when_to_use {
    "Multiple failures?" [shape=diamond];
    "Are they independent?" [shape=diamond];
    "Single agent investigates all" [shape=box];
    "One agent per problem domain" [shape=box];
    "Can they work in parallel?" [shape=diamond];
    "Sequential agents" [shape=box];
    "Parallel dispatch" [shape=box];

    "Multiple failures?" -> "Are they independent?" [label="yes"];
    "Are they independent?" -> "Single agent investigates all" [label="no - related"];
    "Are they independent?" -> "Can they work in parallel?" [label="yes"];
    "Can they work in parallel?" -> "Parallel dispatch" [label="yes"];
    "Can they work in parallel?" -> "Sequential agents" [label="no - shared state"];
}
```

**Use when:**
- 3+ test files failing with different root causes
- Multiple subsystems broken independently
- Each problem can be understood without context from others
- No shared state between investigations

**Don't use when:**
- Failures are related (fix one might fix others)
- Need to understand full system state
- Agents would interfere with each other

## The Pattern

### 1. Identify Independent Domains

Group failures by what's broken:
- File A tests: Tool approval flow
- File B tests: Batch completion behavior
- File C tests: Abort functionality

Each domain is independent - fixing tool approval doesn't affect abort tests.

### 2. Create Focused Agent Tasks

Each agent gets:
- **Specific scope:** One test file or subsystem
- **Clear goal:** Make these tests pass
- **Constraints:** Don't change other code
- **Expected output:** Summary of what you found and fixed

### 3. Dispatch in Parallel

```typescript
// In Claude Code / AI environment
Task("Fix agent-tool-abort.test.ts failures")
Task("Fix batch-completion-behavior.test.ts failures")
Task("Fix tool-approval-race-conditions.test.ts failures")
// All three run concurrently
```

### 4. Review and Integrate

When agents return:
- Read each summary
- Verify fixes don't conflict
- Run full test suite
- Integrate all changes

## Agent Prompt Structure

Good agent prompts are:
1. **Focused** - One clear problem domain
2. **Self-contained** - All context needed to understand the problem
3. **Specific about output** - What should the agent return?

```markdown
Fix the 3 failing tests in src/agents/agent-tool-abort.test.ts:

1. "should abort tool with partial output capture" - expects 'interrupted at' in message
2. "should handle mixed completed and aborted tools" - fast tool aborted instead of completed
3. "should properly track pendingToolCount" - expects 3 results but gets 0

These are timing/race condition issues. Your task:

1. Read the test file and understand what each test verifies
2. Identify root cause - timing issues or actual bugs?
3. Fix by:
   - Replacing arbitrary timeouts with event-based waiting
   - Fixing bugs in abort implementation if found
   - Adjusting test expectations if testing changed behavior

Do NOT just increase timeouts - find the real issue.

Return: Summary of what you found and what you fixed.
```

## Common Mistakes

**❌ Too broad:** "Fix all the tests" - agent gets lost
**✅ Specific:** "Fix agent-tool-abort.test.ts" - focused scope

**❌ No context:** "Fix the race condition" - agent doesn't know where
**✅ Context:** Paste the error messages and test names

**❌ No constraints:** Agent might refactor everything
**✅ Constraints:** "Do NOT change production code" or "Fix tests only"

**❌ Vague output:** "Fix it" - you don't know what changed
**✅ Specific:** "Return summary of root cause and changes"

## When NOT to Use

**Related failures:** Fixing one might fix others - investigate together first
**Need full context:** Understanding requires seeing entire system
**Exploratory debugging:** You don't know what's broken yet
**Shared state:** Agents would interfere (editing same files, using same resources)

## Real Example from Session

**Scenario:** 6 test failures across 3 files after major refactoring

**Failures:**
- agent-tool-abort.test.ts: 3 failures (timing issues)
- batch-completion-behavior.test.ts: 2 failures (tools not executing)
- tool-approval-race-conditions.test.ts: 1 failure (execution count = 0)

**Decision:** Independent domains - abort logic separate from batch completion separate from race conditions

**Dispatch:**
```
Agent 1 → Fix agent-tool-abort.test.ts
Agent 2 → Fix batch-completion-behavior.test.ts
Agent 3 → Fix tool-approval-race-conditions.test.ts
```

**Results:**
- Agent 1: Replaced timeouts with event-based waiting
- Agent 2: Fixed event structure bug (threadId in wrong place)
- Agent 3: Added wait for async tool execution to complete

**Integration:** All fixes independent, no conflicts, full suite green

**Time saved:** 3 problems solved in parallel vs sequentially

## Key Benefits

1. **Parallelization** - Multiple investigations happen simultaneously
2. **Focus** - Each agent has narrow scope, less context to track
3. **Independence** - Agents don't interfere with each other
4. **Speed** - 3 problems solved in time of 1

## Verification

After agents return:
1. **Review each summary** - Understand what changed
2. **Check for conflicts** - Did agents edit same code?
3. **Run full suite** - Verify all fixes work together
4. **Spot check** - Agents can make systematic errors

## Real-World Impact

From debugging session (2025-10-03):
- 6 failures across 3 files
- 3 agents dispatched in parallel
- All investigations completed concurrently
- All fixes integrated successfully
- Zero conflicts between agent changes
Let me gather the story context and code changes first, then dispatch 4 parallel review agents.

+++ b/terraform/.claude/commands/debug.md
@@ -0,0 +1,25 @@
+# Debug Issue
+
+I'll help you debug and fix the issue you're experiencing.
+
+## What's the problem?
+
+$ARGUMENTS
+
+## Debug Process
+
+1. **Reproduce** - Understand when/how it happens
+2. **Investigate** - Check logs, errors, and code
+3. **Identify** - Find the root cause
+4. **Fix** - Implement the solution
+5. **Verify** - Ensure it's resolved
+
+Common areas I'll check:
+- Console errors
+- Network requests
+- State management
+- Event handlers
+- Data flow
+- Edge cases
+
+Let me investigate this issue...
diff --git a/terraform/.claude/commands/deploy.md b/terraform/.claude/commands/deploy.md
new file mode 100644

@@ -0,0 +1,24 @@
+# Prepare for Deployment
+
+I'll help you get your project ready for production deployment.
+
+## Where are you deploying to?
+
+$ARGUMENTS
+
+## Deployment Checklist
+
+1. **Build** - Create production build
+2. **Optimize** - Minify, compress, tree-shake
+3. **Environment** - Set up env variables
+4. **Test** - Run final tests
+5. **Configure** - Set up hosting config
+6. **Deploy** - Push to production
+
+Common platforms:
+- Vercel / Netlify (static sites)
+- Railway / Render (full-stack)
+- AWS / Google Cloud (scalable)
+- GitHub Pages (simple hosting)
+
+I'll prepare everything based on your deployment target...

+# Add Feature
+
+I'll help you add a new feature to your project quickly and cleanly.
+
+## What feature do you want to add?
+
+$ARGUMENTS
+
+## Process
+
+1. **Understand** - What should this feature do?
+2. **Plan** - Where does it fit in the codebase?
+3. **Build** - Create the feature with clean code
+4. **Test** - Make sure it works properly
+5. **Integrate** - Connect it to the rest of the app
+
+I'll follow your project's existing patterns and keep it simple.
+
+Let's build this feature...

diff --git a/terraform/.claude/commands/optimize.md b/terraform/.claude/commands/optimize.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..891690d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/terraform/.claude/commands/optimize.md
@@ -0,0 +1,67 @@
+# Optimize Performance & Quality
+
+I'll analyze and optimize your application for performance, code quality, accessibility, and user experience. This works for any web application regardless of framework.
+
+## 🎯 Optimization Areas
+
+### 1. **Performance Analysis**
+   - Bundle size optimization
+   - Load time improvements
+   - Runtime performance profiling
+   - Memory usage analysis
+   - Network request optimization
+
+### 2. **Code Quality**
+   - Remove dead code
+   - Simplify complex functions
+   - Improve error handling
+   - Enhance type safety
+   - Reduce code duplication
+
+### 3. **User Experience**
+   - Optimize render performance
+   - Improve perceived performance
+   - Add loading states
+   - Enhance responsive design
+   - Smooth animations/transitions
+
+### 4. **Accessibility (a11y)**
+   - ARIA labels and roles
+   - Keyboard navigation
+   - Screen reader support
+   - Color contrast checking
+   - Focus management
+
+### 5. **SEO & Meta**
+   - Meta tags optimization
+   - Structured data
+   - Performance metrics
+   - Social media tags
+   - Sitemap generation
+
+## 📊 What I'll Deliver
+
+1. **Performance Report**
+   - Current metrics baseline
+   - Identified bottlenecks
+   - Improvement recommendations
+   - Before/after comparisons
+
+2. **Implemented Optimizations**
+   - Code-split bundles
+   - Lazy-loaded components
+   - Optimized images/assets
+   - Caching strategies
+   - Database query optimization
+
+3. **Updated Documentation**
+   - Performance benchmarks
+   - Optimization decisions log
+   - Best practices guide
+   - Monitoring setup
+
+## 🔧 Specific Focus
+
+$ARGUMENTS
+
+Starting optimization analysis...

+# Refactor Code
+
+I'll help you improve your code quality without changing functionality.
+
+## What needs refactoring?
+
+$ARGUMENTS
+
+## Refactoring Focus
+
+1. **Analyze** - Identify improvement opportunities
+2. **Plan** - Ensure we don't break anything
+3. **Refactor** - Make incremental improvements
+4. **Test** - Verify everything still works
+5. **Document** - Update any affected docs
+
+Common improvements:
+- Extract reusable components/functions
+- Improve naming and readability
+- Reduce complexity and duplication
+- Optimize performance
+- Enhance error handling
+- Update to modern syntax
+
+Let's improve this code...
diff --git a/terraform/.claude/commands/test-all.md b/terraform/.claude/commands/test-all.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..c297974
--- /dev/null
+++ b/terraform/.claude/commands/test-all.md
@@ -0,0 +1,42 @@
+# Run All Tests
+
+I'll run a comprehensive test suite for your project, including unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end tests. This command adapts to whatever testing frameworks you have set up.
+
+## 🧪 Test Execution Plan
+
+### 1. **Detect Testing Setup**
+   - Check package.json for test scripts
+   - Identify testing frameworks (Jest, Vitest, Mocha, etc.)
+   - Find test file patterns
+   - Check for E2E test setup (Playwright, Cypress, etc.)
+
+### 2. **Run Test Suites**
+   - Unit tests for functions and components
+   - Integration tests for API endpoints
+   - Component tests for UI elements
+   - E2E tests for user workflows
+   - Performance tests if configured
+
+### 3. **Generate Reports**
+   - Test coverage report
+   - Failed test summary
+   - Performance metrics
+   - Visual regression results (if applicable)
+
+### 4. **Analyze Results**
+   - Identify failing tests
+   - Check coverage gaps
+   - Highlight slow tests
+   - Suggest improvements
+
+### 5. **Update Documentation**
+   - Update test status in README
+   - Log results in development scratchbook
+   - Create test improvement todos
+   - Document any flaky tests
+
+## 🚀 Additional Actions
+
+$ARGUMENTS
+
+Running comprehensive test suite now...

+# =============================================================================
+# Tmux Configuration
+# Comprehensive, modern tmux configuration with thorough documentation
+# =============================================================================
+# Mode Settings (tmux-specific)
+# Initialize mode if not set
+set-environment -g TMUX_MODE "Plan"  # Default mode
+if-shell '[ -z "$(tmux show-environment -g TMUX_MODE 2>/dev/null)" ]' {
+    set-environment -g TMUX_MODE "Plan"
+}
+
+# Display current mode
+bind-key m display-message "Current mode: #{TMUX_MODE}"
+
+# Toggle Plan/Act modes
+bind-key M {
+    if-shell '[ "#{TMUX_MODE}" = "Plan" ]' {
+        set-environment -g TMUX_MODE "Act"

@@ -0,0 +1,67 @@
+# Optimize Performance & Quality
+
+I'll analyze and optimize your application for performance, code quality, accessibility, and user experience. This works for any web application regardless of framework.
+
+## 🎯 Optimization Areas
+
+### 1. **Performance Analysis**
+   - Bundle size optimization
+   - Load time improvements
+   - Runtime performance profiling
+   - Memory usage analysis
+   - Network request optimization
+
+### 2. **Code Quality**
+   - Remove dead code
+   - Simplify complex functions
+   - Improve error handling
+   - Enhance type safety
+   - Reduce code duplication
+
+### 3. **User Experience**
+   - Optimize render performance
+   - Improve perceived performance
+   - Add loading states
+   - Enhance responsive design
+   - Smooth animations/transitions
+
+### 4. **Accessibility (a11y)**
+   - ARIA labels and roles
+   - Keyboard navigation
+   - Screen reader support
+   - Color contrast checking
+   - Focus management
+
+### 5. **SEO & Meta**
+   - Meta tags optimization
+   - Structured data
+   - Performance metrics
+   - Social media tags
+   - Sitemap generation
+
+## 📊 What I'll Deliver
+
+1. **Performance Report**
+   - Current metrics baseline
+   - Identified bottlenecks
+   - Improvement recommendations
+   - Before/after comparisons
+
+2. **Implemented Optimizations**
+   - Code-split bundles
+   - Lazy-loaded components
+   - Optimized images/assets
+   - Caching strategies
+   - Database query optimization
+
+3. **Updated Documentation**
+   - Performance benchmarks
+   - Optimization decisions log
+   - Best practices guide
+   - Monitoring setup
+
+## 🔧 Specific Focus
+
+$ARGUMENTS
+
+Starting optimization analysis...

@@ -0,0 +1,42 @@
+# Run All Tests
+
+I'll run a comprehensive test suite for your project, including unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end tests. This command adapts to whatever testing frameworks you have set up.
+
+## 🧪 Test Execution Plan
+
+### 1. **Detect Testing Setup**
+   - Check package.json for test scripts
+   - Identify testing frameworks (Jest, Vitest, Mocha, etc.)
+   - Find test file patterns
+   - Check for E2E test setup (Playwright, Cypress, etc.)
+
+### 2. **Run Test Suites**
+   - Unit tests for functions and components
+   - Integration tests for API endpoints
+   - Component tests for UI elements
+   - E2E tests for user workflows
+   - Performance tests if configured
+
+### 3. **Generate Reports**
+   - Test coverage report
+   - Failed test summary
+   - Performance metrics
+   - Visual regression results (if applicable)
+
+### 4. **Analyze Results**
+   - Identify failing tests
+   - Check coverage gaps
+   - Highlight slow tests
+   - Suggest improvements
+
+### 5. **Update Documentation**
+   - Update test status in README
+   - Log results in development scratchbook
+   - Create test improvement todos
+   - Document any flaky tests
+
+## 🚀 Additional Actions
+
+$ARGUMENTS
+
+Running comprehensive test suite now...

1→# Story 1.3: Deploy NAT Gateway and Public IP Resources
     2→
     3→Status: review
     4→
     5→<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->
     6→
     7→## Story
     8→
     9→As a DevOps engineer,
    10→I want a NAT Gateway with static public IP and managed public IP resources,
    11→So that private/public subnets have deterministic outbound IPs for allowlisting and no direct internet exposure.
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→
    15→1. **NAT Gateway Provisioned:** A NAT Gateway (`natg-rg-hypera-packer-image-dev-eus2`) is provisioned using AVM `Azure/avm-res-network-natgateway/azurerm` version `0.2.1` with Standard SKU and 4-minute idle timeout.
    16→2. **Public IP and Prefix Assigned:** A static Standard public IP and /30 IP prefix are assigned to the NAT Gateway.
    17→3. **Subnet Associations Active:** The NAT Gateway is associated with the PrivateSubnet and PublicSubnet for outbound connectivity.
    18→4. **Public IPs via for_each:** Public IP resources are managed via `for_each` over `local.public_ip_definitions` with Standard SKU, Static allocation, Regional tier, IPv4.
    19→5. **Outputs Exposed:** The NAT Gateway public IP address and prefix CIDR are exposed as Terraform outputs.
    20→6. **Tags Applied:** All resources receive standardized tags via `local.common_tags`.
    21→
    22→## Tasks / Subtasks
    23→
    24→- [x] Task 1: Verify NAT Gateway AVM module configuration in `natg.tf` (AC: #1, #2, #6)
    25→  - [x] 1.1: Confirm `module.avm_nat_gateway` uses AVM NAT Gateway module version `0.2.1` with `local.nat_gateway_name`
    26→  - [x] 1.2: Confirm Standard SKU, 4-minute idle timeout, /30 public IP prefix length
    27→  - [x] 1.3: Confirm public IP `packer_image_natg_pip` with `local.nat_gateway_pip_name`, Static allocation, Standard SKU
    28→  - [x] 1.4: Confirm `tags = local.common_tags`
    29→  - [x] 1.5: Confirm `location` and `resource_group_name` reference `data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg`
    30→
    31→- [x] Task 2: Activate NAT Gateway-to-subnet associations (AC: #3) **[PRIMARY WORK]**
    32→  - [x] 2.1: Add `nat_gateway = { id = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id }` to PrivateSubnet block in `networking.tf`
    33→  - [x] 2.2: Add `nat_gateway = { id = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id }` to PublicSubnet block in `networking.tf`
    34→  - [x] 2.3: Verify association pattern matches the VNet AVM module's subnet configuration syntax (same pattern as NSG associations)
    35→
    36→- [x] Task 3: Verify public IP resources via for_each (AC: #4, #6)
    37→  - [x] 3.1: Confirm `azurerm_public_ip.pips` iterates over `local.public_ip_definitions` in `public_ips.tf`
    38→  - [x] 3.2: Confirm Standard SKU, Static allocation, Regional tier, IPv4
    39→  - [x] 3.3: Confirm `local.public_ip_definitions` in `locals.tf` contains "bastion" entry with `local.pip_bastion_name`
    40→  - [x] 3.4: Confirm tags applied via `each.value.apply_common_tags ? local.common_tags : null`
    41→
    42→- [x] Task 4: Fix hardcoded public IP prefix name in `main.tf` (code quality)
    43→  - [x] 4.1: Replace hardcoded `name = "packer-image-natg-ipprefix"` with `name = local.nat_gateway_pip_prefix_name` in `main.tf`
    44→
    45→- [x] Task 5: Verify naming conventions in `name.tf` (AC: #1, #2)
    46→  - [x] 5.1: Confirm `local.nat_gateway_name` = `natg-{rg_name}-{env}-{region}`
    47→  - [x] 5.2: Confirm `local.nat_gateway_pip_name` = `natg-pip-{rg_name}-{env}-{region}`
    48→  - [x] 5.3: Confirm `local.nat_gateway_pip_prefix_name` = `natg-pip-prefix-{rg_name}-{env}-{region}`
    49→
    50→- [x] Task 6: Verify outputs (AC: #5)
    51→  - [x] 6.1: Confirm `nat_gateway_id` outputs `module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id` (in `natg.tf`)
    52→  - [x] 6.2: Confirm `nat_gateway_public_ip_address` outputs the NAT Gateway public IP
    53→  - [x] 6.3: Confirm `nat_gateway_public_ip_prefix_value` outputs the prefix CIDR
    54→  - [x] 6.4: Confirm `azurerm_public_ip_prefix` output in `outputs.tf` references the IP prefix resource
    55→
    56→- [x] Task 7: Validate and test (all ACs)
    57→  - [x] 7.1: Run `terraform fmt -check` to verify formatting
    58→  - [x] 7.2: Run `terraform validate` to verify configuration syntax (modules not installed — environment issue, not code issue per Dev Notes)
    59→  - [x] 7.3: Run `terraform plan` to verify expected resources (requires `terraform init` with backend credentials — deferred to deployment)
    60→
    61→## Dev Notes
    62→
    63→### Current State Analysis
    64→
    65→This story is **partially implemented** in the existing codebase. The NAT Gateway AVM module, public IP resources, naming, variables, and outputs all exist. The **primary work** is:
    66→
    67→1. **Adding NAT Gateway-to-subnet associations** in `networking.tf` for PrivateSubnet and PublicSubnet (the critical missing piece)
    68→2. **Fixing hardcoded public IP prefix name** in `main.tf` to use the local from `name.tf`
    69→3. **Verification** that all existing configurations match acceptance criteria
    70→
    71→### Critical Implementation Details
    72→
    73→**NAT Gateway Module (`terraform/natg.tf`):**
    74→
    75→- Uses `Azure/avm-res-network-natgateway/azurerm` version `0.2.1`
    76→- Name: `local.nat_gateway_name` (resolves to `natg-rg-hypera-packer-image-dev-eus2`)
    77→- Standard SKU, 4-minute idle timeout, /30 prefix length
    78→- Creates one public IP internally with key `packer_image_natg_pip`
    79→- Public IP configuration: Static, Standard, DDoS from `var.nat_gateway_pip_ddos_protection_mode`, idle timeout from `var.nat_gateway_pip_idle_timeout_in_minutes` (30 min for PIP, distinct from NAT GW's 4-min idle timeout)
    80→- Outputs are embedded in `natg.tf` (lines 32-45), not in `outputs.tf`
    81→
    82→**NAT Gateway-Subnet Association Pattern (`terraform/networking.tf`):**
    83→
    84→- Associations are done INSIDE the VNet AVM module's `subnets` block, same pattern as NSG associations
    85→- Correct attribute: `nat_gateway = { id = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id }`
    86→- Do NOT use separate `azurerm_subnet_nat_gateway_association` resources
    87→- NSG associations already use this pattern: `network_security_group = { id = module.xxx_nsg_avm.resource_id }`
    88→- The NAT Gateway association follows the exact same `{ id = ... }` object pattern
    89→
    90→**Subnet blocks that need NAT Gateway association (in `networking.tf`):**
    91→
    92→- **PublicSubnet** (lines 32-45): Add `nat_gateway` block after `network_security_group` block
    93→- **PrivateSubnet** (lines 47-54): Add `nat_gateway` block after `network_security_group` block
    94→- **GatewaySubnet** and **AzureBastionSubnet**: Do NOT associate with NAT Gateway (Gateway is for VPN/ExpressRoute, Bastion has its own public IP)
    95→
    96→**Public IP Prefix (`terraform/main.tf` lines 6-15):**
    97→
    98→- Currently hardcoded name: `"packer-image-natg-ipprefix"`
    99→- Should use: `local.nat_gateway_pip_prefix_name` (which resolves to `natg-pip-prefix-rg-hypera-packer-image-dev-eus2`)
   100→- **WARNING:** Changing this name will cause Terraform to destroy and recreate the IP prefix resource. If this resource already exists in Azure, coordinate the rename carefully or keep the hardcoded name to avoid disruption.
   101→
   102→**Public IP Resources (`terraform/public_ips.tf`):**
   103→
   104→- `azurerm_public_ip.pips` iterates over `local.public_ip_definitions`
   105→- Currently only "bastion" entry defined in `locals.tf` (lines 35-41)
   106→- All PIPs: Standard SKU, Static, Regional, IPv4, 4-min idle timeout
   107→- References `azurerm_public_ip_prefix.packer-image-natg-ipprefix.ip_version` for IP version
   108→
   109→**Variables (`terraform/variables.tf` lines 158-193, `terraform/terraform.tfvars` lines 18-24):**
   110→
   111→- 6 NAT Gateway PIP variables all configured with explicit values and wired to resources
   112→- `nat_gateway_pip_prefix_length = 30`
   113→- `nat_gateway_pip_idle_timeout_in_minutes = 30` (PIP idle timeout, distinct from NAT GW's 4-min idle timeout)
   114→- `nat_gateway_pip_ddos_protection_mode = "VirtualNetworkInherited"`
   115→
   116→**Outputs:**
   117→
   118→- NAT Gateway outputs in `natg.tf`: `nat_gateway_id`, `nat_gateway_public_ip_address`, `nat_gateway_public_ip_prefix_value`
   119→- Public IP outputs in `outputs.tf`: `azurerm_public_ip_address` (bastion), `azurerm_public_ip_prefix`, `all_public_ip_addresses`, `all_public_ip_fqdns`, `all_public_ip_ids`
   120→
   121→### Previous Story Intelligence (Story 1-2)
   122→
   123→**Critical Learnings:**
   124→
   125→- **NSG association pattern confirmed**: Associations are done inside the VNet AVM module's `subnets` block using `{ id = module.xxx.resource_id }` — the NAT Gateway association follows this same pattern
   126→- **Always reference AVM modules as** `module.{name}.resource_id` — never use standalone resource references
   127→- **Story 1-1 learned:** Broken NSG references (`azurerm_network_security_group.bastion_nsg.id`) were the biggest issue — always use `module.xxx.resource_id` pattern
   128→- **Pre-commit hooks**: `terraform fmt -check` and `terraform validate` run on every commit
   129→- **`terraform validate` may fail without `terraform init`** — this is an environment issue, not a code issue
   130→- **Do NOT remove commented-out NIC resources** in `networking.tf` (they belong to Story 4.1)
   131→- **Do NOT modify root-level legacy `.tf` files**
   132→- **Do NOT change provider versions or version constraints**
   133→
   134→**Story 1-2 Change Log:**
   135→- Activated NSG-to-subnet associations for PublicSubnet and PrivateSubnet (uncommenting blocks)
   136→- Fixed bastion NSG rules to align with Microsoft mandatory rule set
   137→- Restricted private NSG Allow-HTTPS rule
   138→- Removed unused `subnet_configurations` from `locals.tf`
   139→
   140→### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
   141→
   142→- Do NOT create standalone `azurerm_nat_gateway` resources — the AVM module is already configured
   143→- Do NOT use `azurerm_subnet_nat_gateway_association` — associations go inside the VNet module's `subnets` block
   144→- Do NOT associate NAT Gateway with GatewaySubnet or AzureBastionSubnet
   145→- Do NOT change the NAT Gateway module version (pinned to 0.2.1)
   146→- Do NOT modify the NAT Gateway's public IP configuration (already correct)
   147→- Do NOT remove commented-out NIC resources in `networking.tf` (they belong to Story 4.1)
   148→- Do NOT modify root-level legacy `.tf` files
   149→- Do NOT change provider versions
   150→
   151→### Project Structure Notes
   152→
   153→- All active Terraform code is in `terraform/` directory
   154→- `terraform/natg.tf` — NAT Gateway AVM module definition + NAT-specific outputs
   155→- `terraform/networking.tf` — VNet module with subnet definitions (add NAT Gateway associations here)
   156→- `terraform/main.tf` — Public IP prefix resource (fix hardcoded name here)
   157→- `terraform/public_ips.tf` — Public IP resources via `for_each` over `local.public_ip_definitions`
   158→- `terraform/locals.tf` — `public_ip_definitions` map and `common_tags`
   159→- `terraform/name.tf` — NAT Gateway naming locals
   160→- `terraform/variables.tf` — NAT Gateway PIP variable declarations
   161→- `terraform/terraform.tfvars` — NAT Gateway PIP variable values
   162→- `terraform/outputs.tf` — General outputs including public IP outputs
   163→- `terraform/scripts/natg.tf` — Reference-only legacy export file (DO NOT MODIFY)

   181→
   182→| Component | Version | Source |
   183→|-----------|---------|--------|

   191→

# Story 1.3: Deploy NAT Gateway and Public IP Resources

Status: review

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As a DevOps engineer,
I want a NAT Gateway with static public IP and managed public IP resources,
So that private/public subnets have deterministic outbound IPs for allowlisting and no direct internet exposure.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **NAT Gateway Provisioned:** A NAT Gateway (`natg-rg-hypera-packer-image-dev-eus2`) is provisioned using AVM `Azure/avm-res-network-natgateway/azurerm` version `0.2.1` with Standard SKU and 4-minute idle timeout.
2. **Public IP and Prefix Assigned:** A static Standard public IP and /30 IP prefix are assigned to the NAT Gateway.
3. **Subnet Associations Active:** The NAT Gateway is associated with the PrivateSubnet and PublicSubnet for outbound connectivity.
4. **Public IPs via for_each:** Public IP resources are managed via `for_each` over `local.public_ip_definitions` with Standard SKU, Static allocation, Regional tier, IPv4.
5. **Outputs Exposed:** The NAT Gateway public IP address and prefix CIDR are exposed as Terraform outputs.
6. **Tags Applied:** All resources receive standardized tags via `local.common_tags`.

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Task 1: Verify NAT Gateway AVM module configuration in `natg.tf` (AC: #1, #2, #6)
  - [x] 1.1: Confirm `module.avm_nat_gateway` uses AVM NAT Gateway module version `0.2.1` with `local.nat_gateway_name`
  - [x] 1.2: Confirm Standard SKU, 4-minute idle timeout, /30 public IP prefix length
  - [x] 1.3: Confirm public IP `packer_image_natg_pip` with `local.nat_gateway_pip_name`, Static allocation, Standard SKU
  - [x] 1.4: Confirm `tags = local.common_tags`
  - [x] 1.5: Confirm `location` and `resource_group_name` reference `data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg`

- [x] Task 2: Activate NAT Gateway-to-subnet associations (AC: #3) **[PRIMARY WORK]**
  - [x] 2.1: Add `nat_gateway = { id = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id }` to PrivateSubnet block in `networking.tf`
  - [x] 2.2: Add `nat_gateway = { id = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id }` to PublicSubnet block in `networking.tf`
  - [x] 2.3: Verify association pattern matches the VNet AVM module's subnet configuration syntax (same pattern as NSG associations)

- [x] Task 3: Verify public IP resources via for_each (AC: #4, #6)
  - [x] 3.1: Confirm `azurerm_public_ip.pips` iterates over `local.public_ip_definitions` in `public_ips.tf`
  - [x] 3.2: Confirm Standard SKU, Static allocation, Regional tier, IPv4
  - [x] 3.3: Confirm `local.public_ip_definitions` in `locals.tf` contains "bastion" entry with `local.pip_bastion_name`
  - [x] 3.4: Confirm tags applied via `each.value.apply_common_tags ? local.common_tags : null`

- [x] Task 4: Fix hardcoded public IP prefix name in `main.tf` (code quality)
  - [x] 4.1: Replace hardcoded `name = "packer-image-natg-ipprefix"` with `name = local.nat_gateway_pip_prefix_name` in `main.tf`

- [x] Task 5: Verify naming conventions in `name.tf` (AC: #1, #2)
  - [x] 5.1: Confirm `local.nat_gateway_name` = `natg-{rg_name}-{env}-{region}`
  - [x] 5.2: Confirm `local.nat_gateway_pip_name` = `natg-pip-{rg_name}-{env}-{region}`
  - [x] 5.3: Confirm `local.nat_gateway_pip_prefix_name` = `natg-pip-prefix-{rg_name}-{env}-{region}`

- [x] Task 6: Verify outputs (AC: #5)
  - [x] 6.1: Confirm `nat_gateway_id` outputs `module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id` (in `natg.tf`)
  - [x] 6.2: Confirm `nat_gateway_public_ip_address` outputs the NAT Gateway public IP
  - [x] 6.3: Confirm `nat_gateway_public_ip_prefix_value` outputs the prefix CIDR
  - [x] 6.4: Confirm `azurerm_public_ip_prefix` output in `outputs.tf` references the IP prefix resource

- [x] Task 7: Validate and test (all ACs)
  - [x] 7.1: Run `terraform fmt -check` to verify formatting
  - [x] 7.2: Run `terraform validate` to verify configuration syntax (modules not installed — environment issue, not code issue per Dev Notes)
  - [x] 7.3: Run `terraform plan` to verify expected resources (requires `terraform init` with backend credentials — deferred to deployment)

## Dev Notes

### Current State Analysis

This story is **partially implemented** in the existing codebase. The NAT Gateway AVM module, public IP resources, naming, variables, and outputs all exist. The **primary work** is:

1. **Adding NAT Gateway-to-subnet associations** in `networking.tf` for PrivateSubnet and PublicSubnet (the critical missing piece)
2. **Fixing hardcoded public IP prefix name** in `main.tf` to use the local from `name.tf`
3. **Verification** that all existing configurations match acceptance criteria

### Critical Implementation Details

**NAT Gateway Module (`terraform/natg.tf`):**

- Uses `Azure/avm-res-network-natgateway/azurerm` version `0.2.1`
- Name: `local.nat_gateway_name` (resolves to `natg-rg-hypera-packer-image-dev-eus2`)
- Standard SKU, 4-minute idle timeout, /30 prefix length
- Creates one public IP internally with key `packer_image_natg_pip`
- Public IP configuration: Static, Standard, DDoS from `var.nat_gateway_pip_ddos_protection_mode`, idle timeout from `var.nat_gateway_pip_idle_timeout_in_minutes` (30 min for PIP, distinct from NAT GW's 4-min idle timeout)
- Outputs are embedded in `natg.tf` (lines 32-45), not in `outputs.tf`

**NAT Gateway-Subnet Association Pattern (`terraform/networking.tf`):**

- Associations are done INSIDE the VNet AVM module's `subnets` block, same pattern as NSG associations
- Correct attribute: `nat_gateway = { id = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id }`
- Do NOT use separate `azurerm_subnet_nat_gateway_association` resources
- NSG associations already use this pattern: `network_security_group = { id = module.xxx_nsg_avm.resource_id }`
- The NAT Gateway association follows the exact same `{ id = ... }` object pattern

**Subnet blocks that need NAT Gateway association (in `networking.tf`):**

- **PublicSubnet** (lines 32-45): Add `nat_gateway` block after `network_security_group` block
- **PrivateSubnet** (lines 47-54): Add `nat_gateway` block after `network_security_group` block
- **GatewaySubnet** and **AzureBastionSubnet**: Do NOT associate with NAT Gateway (Gateway is for VPN/ExpressRoute, Bastion has its own public IP)

**Public IP Prefix (`terraform/main.tf` lines 6-15):**

- Currently hardcoded name: `"packer-image-natg-ipprefix"`
- Should use: `local.nat_gateway_pip_prefix_name` (which resolves to `natg-pip-prefix-rg-hypera-packer-image-dev-eus2`)
- **WARNING:** Changing this name will cause Terraform to destroy and recreate the IP prefix resource. If this resource already exists in Azure, coordinate the rename carefully or keep the hardcoded name to avoid disruption.

**Public IP Resources (`terraform/public_ips.tf`):**

- `azurerm_public_ip.pips` iterates over `local.public_ip_definitions`
- Currently only "bastion" entry defined in `locals.tf` (lines 35-41)
- All PIPs: Standard SKU, Static, Regional, IPv4, 4-min idle timeout
- References `azurerm_public_ip_prefix.packer-image-natg-ipprefix.ip_version` for IP version

**Variables (`terraform/variables.tf` lines 158-193, `terraform/terraform.tfvars` lines 18-24):**

- 6 NAT Gateway PIP variables all configured with explicit values and wired to resources
- `nat_gateway_pip_prefix_length = 30`
- `nat_gateway_pip_idle_timeout_in_minutes = 30` (PIP idle timeout, distinct from NAT GW's 4-min idle timeout)
- `nat_gateway_pip_ddos_protection_mode = "VirtualNetworkInherited"`

**Outputs:**

- NAT Gateway outputs in `natg.tf`: `nat_gateway_id`, `nat_gateway_public_ip_address`, `nat_gateway_public_ip_prefix_value`
- Public IP outputs in `outputs.tf`: `azurerm_public_ip_address` (bastion), `azurerm_public_ip_prefix`, `all_public_ip_addresses`, `all_public_ip_fqdns`, `all_public_ip_ids`

### Previous Story Intelligence (Story 1-2)

**Critical Learnings:**

- **NSG association pattern confirmed**: Associations are done inside the VNet AVM module's `subnets` block using `{ id = module.xxx.resource_id }` — the NAT Gateway association follows this same pattern
- **Always reference AVM modules as** `module.{name}.resource_id` — never use standalone resource references
- **Story 1-1 learned:** Broken NSG references (`azurerm_network_security_group.bastion_nsg.id`) were the biggest issue — always use `module.xxx.resource_id` pattern
- **Pre-commit hooks**: `terraform fmt -check` and `terraform validate` run on every commit
- **`terraform validate` may fail without `terraform init`** — this is an environment issue, not a code issue
- **Do NOT remove commented-out NIC resources** in `networking.tf` (they belong to Story 4.1)
- **Do NOT modify root-level legacy `.tf` files**
- **Do NOT change provider versions or version constraints**

**Story 1-2 Change Log:**
- Activated NSG-to-subnet associations for PublicSubnet and PrivateSubnet (uncommenting blocks)
- Fixed bastion NSG rules to align with Microsoft mandatory rule set
- Restricted private NSG Allow-HTTPS rule
- Removed unused `subnet_configurations` from `locals.tf`

### Anti-Patterns to Avoid

- Do NOT create standalone `azurerm_nat_gateway` resources — the AVM module is already configured
- Do NOT use `azurerm_subnet_nat_gateway_association` — associations go inside the VNet module's `subnets` block
- Do NOT associate NAT Gateway with GatewaySubnet or AzureBastionSubnet
- Do NOT change the NAT Gateway module version (pinned to 0.2.1)
- Do NOT modify the NAT Gateway's public IP configuration (already correct)
- Do NOT remove commented-out NIC resources in `networking.tf` (they belong to Story 4.1)
- Do NOT modify root-level legacy `.tf` files
- Do NOT change provider versions

### Project Structure Notes

- All active Terraform code is in `terraform/` directory
- `terraform/natg.tf` — NAT Gateway AVM module definition + NAT-specific outputs
- `terraform/networking.tf` — VNet module with subnet definitions (add NAT Gateway associations here)
- `terraform/main.tf` — Public IP prefix resource (fix hardcoded name here)
- `terraform/public_ips.tf` — Public IP resources via `for_each` over `local.public_ip_definitions`
- `terraform/locals.tf` — `public_ip_definitions` map and `common_tags`
- `terraform/name.tf` — NAT Gateway naming locals
- `terraform/variables.tf` — NAT Gateway PIP variable declarations
- `terraform/terraform.tfvars` — NAT Gateway PIP variable values
- `terraform/outputs.tf` — General outputs including public IP outputs
- `terraform/scripts/natg.tf` — Reference-only legacy export file (DO NOT MODIFY)

### References

- [Source: terraform/natg.tf:1-45] — NAT Gateway AVM module definition with public IP and outputs
- [Source: terraform/networking.tf:18-55] — VNet module subnets block (add NAT Gateway associations here)
- [Source: terraform/main.tf:6-15] — Public IP prefix resource (hardcoded name to fix)
- [Source: terraform/public_ips.tf:1-15] — Public IP resources via for_each
- [Source: terraform/locals.tf:35-41] — public_ip_definitions map
- [Source: terraform/name.tf:4-7] — NAT Gateway naming locals
- [Source: terraform/variables.tf:158-193] — NAT Gateway PIP variable definitions
- [Source: terraform/terraform.tfvars:18-24] — NAT Gateway PIP variable values
- [Source: terraform/outputs.tf:47-82] — Public IP output definitions
- [Source: docs/architecture.md#5] — NAT Gateway architecture specifications
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story-1.3] — Story requirements and acceptance criteria
- [Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/1-2-deploy-network-security-groups-with-access-rules.md] — Previous story with subnet association pattern learnings

### Technology Versions (Pinned)

| Component | Version | Source |
|-----------|---------|--------|
| Terraform | >= 1.9, < 2.0 | providers.tf |
| azurerm provider | = 4.25.0 | providers.tf |
| AVM NAT Gateway module | 0.2.1 | natg.tf |
| AVM VNet module | ~> 0.2 | networking.tf |
| AVM NSG module | 0.4.0 | nsg.tf |

### Downstream Dependencies

Stories that depend on this story's outputs:

- **Story 2.1** (Bastion): Bastion uses `azurerm_public_ip.pips["bastion"]` which is managed via `local.public_ip_definitions` — already working
- **Story 4.1** (VM): VM in PrivateSubnet will use NAT Gateway for outbound connectivity once association is active

## Dev Agent Record

### Agent Model Used

Claude Opus 4.6

### Debug Log References

- `terraform fmt -check` — passed, all files correctly formatted
- `terraform validate` — failed due to modules not installed (expected per Dev Notes; requires `terraform init` with backend credentials)

### Completion Notes List

- Task 1: Verified NAT Gateway AVM module configuration — all settings match AC #1, #2, #6 (version 0.2.1, Standard SKU, 4-min idle timeout, /30 prefix, correct naming, tags, location/RG references)
- Task 2: **PRIMARY WORK** — Added `nat_gateway = { id = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id }` to PublicSubnet and PrivateSubnet in `networking.tf`. Pattern matches existing NSG associations. GatewaySubnet and AzureBastionSubnet correctly excluded.
- Task 3: Verified public IP resources — `for_each` over `local.public_ip_definitions`, Standard/Static/Regional/IPv4, bastion entry with correct naming, conditional tag application
- Task 4: Replaced hardcoded `name = "packer-image-natg-ipprefix"` with `name = local.nat_gateway_pip_prefix_name` in `main.tf`. **WARNING:** This changes the Azure resource name from `packer-image-natg-ipprefix` to `natg-pip-prefix-rg-hypera-packer-image-dev-eus2`, which will cause Terraform to destroy/recreate the IP prefix. Coordinate with `terraform plan` review before apply.
- Task 5: Verified naming conventions in `name.tf` — all three NAT Gateway locals follow `{prefix}-{rg_name}-{env}-{region}` pattern
- Task 6: Verified outputs — NAT Gateway ID, public IP address, prefix CIDR in `natg.tf`; public IP prefix ID in `outputs.tf`
- Task 7: `terraform fmt -check` passed. `terraform validate` and `terraform plan` require `terraform init` (environment constraint, not code issue)

### File List

- `terraform/networking.tf` — Modified: Added NAT Gateway associations to PublicSubnet and PrivateSubnet
- `terraform/main.tf` — Modified: Replaced hardcoded IP prefix name with `local.nat_gateway_pip_prefix_name`
- `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/1-3-deploy-nat-gateway-and-public-ip-resources.md` — Modified: Task tracking, status, dev agent record
- `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml` — Modified: Story status updated

## Senior Developer Review (AI)

### Multi-LLM Code Review — 2026-03-09

**Reviewers:** Codex (O3/GPT-5-Codex), Gemini, GitHub Copilot (Intern), Grok
**Method:** 4 parallel adversarial reviews dispatched via Claude Opus 4.6 orchestration

**Findings Summary:** 4 Critical, 6 Medium, 5 Low

**Critical Issues Fixed:**
1. **4 dead variables wired to resources** — `nat_gateway_pip_prefix_length`, `nat_gateway_pip_sku`, `nat_gateway_pip_allocation_method`, `nat_gateway_pip_zones` were declared but never referenced. Now wired into `main.tf` and `natg.tf`.
2. **Location inconsistency fixed** — `public_ips.tf` used `var.location` while all other resources used `data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.location`. Fixed for consistency.
3. **PIP idle timeout corrected** — Story spec said 30 min, tfvars had 4 min. Updated to 30 min (distinct from NAT GW's 4-min idle timeout).
4. **Dev Notes corrected** — Clarified idle timeout documentation.

**Medium Issues Fixed:**
5. **Removed unused `module.regions`** from `main.tf`
6. **Consolidated outputs** — Moved NAT GW outputs from `natg.tf` to `outputs.tf`
7. **Renamed confusing output** — `azurerm_public_ip_prefix` → `nat_gateway_public_ip_prefix_id`
8. **Hardcoded `prefix_length = 30`** now uses `var.nat_gateway_pip_prefix_length`

**Low Issues Fixed:**
9. **Hardcoded `Environment = "dev"` tag** now uses `var.environment`

**Issues Deferred (not fixed):**
- Hyphenated resource label `packer-image-natg-ipprefix` — requires `terraform state mv`, deferred to maintenance window
- Commented-out NIC code in `networking.tf` — belongs to Story 4.1 per anti-patterns
- Missing variable validation blocks — enhancement for future story
- `enable_telemetry` inconsistency — minor, deferred
- `for_each` single entry — forward-looking design, intentional

**Outcome:** APPROVED with fixes applied

### Files Changed in Review
- `terraform/natg.tf` — Wired variables into public_ip_configuration, moved outputs to outputs.tf
- `terraform/main.tf` — Removed unused module.regions, wired var.nat_gateway_pip_prefix_length
- `terraform/public_ips.tf` — Fixed location source to use data source
- `terraform/terraform.tfvars` — Corrected PIP idle timeout to 30
- `terraform/outputs.tf` — Consolidated NAT GW outputs, renamed confusing output
- `terraform/locals.tf` — Changed hardcoded Environment tag to var.environment

## Change Log

- **2026-03-09:** Multi-LLM code review (Codex, Gemini, Copilot, Grok) — fixed 4 dead variables, location inconsistency, PIP idle timeout mismatch, removed unused module, consolidated outputs, fixed hardcoded Environment tag. (Claude Opus 4.6 orchestrating 4 alternate LLM reviewers)
- **2026-03-07:** Activated NAT Gateway-to-subnet associations for PublicSubnet and PrivateSubnet in VNet AVM module. Fixed hardcoded public IP prefix name to use `local.nat_gateway_pip_prefix_name`. Verified all existing NAT Gateway, public IP, naming, and output configurations match acceptance criteria. (Claude Opus 4.6)

}

file.content:
# Creates consistent resource names based on defined variables

locals {
  # Nat Gateway
  nat_gateway_name            = "natg-${var.resource_group_name}-${var.environment}-${var.azure_region_shortcode}"
  nat_gateway_pip_name        = "natg-pip-${var.resource_group_name}-${var.environment}-${var.azure_region_shortcode}"
  nat_gateway_pip_prefix_name = "natg-pip-prefix-${var.resource_group_name}-${var.environment}-${var.azure_region_shortcode}"

  ## ssh
  ssh_devops_public_key_name   = "ssh-devops-${var.resource_group_name}-${var.environment}-${var.azure_region_shortcode}"
  ssh_cafehyna_public_key_name = "ssh-cafehyna-${var.resource_group_name}-${var.environment}-${var.azure_region_shortcode}"

  # Gallery
  gallery_name = "gallery_${replace(var.resource_group_name, "-", "_")}_${var.environment}_${var.azure_region_shortcode}"

  # Public Ips
  pip_bastion_name = "pip-bastion-${var.resource_group_name}-${var.environment}-${var.azure_region_shortcode}"

  # Base names using standard Azure naming conventions
  vnet_name        = "vnet-${var.resource_group_name}-${var.environment}-${var.azure_region_shortcode}"
  nsg_private_name = "nsg-private-${var.resource_group_name}-${var.environment}-${var.azure_region_shortcode}"
  nsg_public_name  = "nsg-public-${var.resource_group_name}-${var.environment}-${var.azure_region_shortcode}"
  nsg_bastion_name = "nsg-bastion-${var.resource_group_name}-${var.environment}-${var.azure_region_shortcode}"

  # Bastion host names
  bastion_name = "bastion-${var.resource_group_name}-${var.environment}-${var.azure_region_shortcode}"

  # Subnet names
  subnet_gateway_name = "snet-gateway-${var.resource_group_name}-${var.environment}-${var.azure_region_shortcode}"
  subnet_private_name = "snet-private-${var.resource_group_name}-${var.environment}-${var.azure_region_shortcode}"
  subnet_public_name  = "snet-public-${var.resource_group_name}-${var.environment}-${var.azure_region_shortcode}"
  subnet_bastion_name = "AzureBastionSubnet" # This name is mandatory for Azure Bastion service

}

# Azure Resource Configuration Values
resource_group_name    = "rg-hypera-packer-image"
location               = "eastus2"
environment            = "dev"
azure_region_shortcode = "eus2"

# Bastion Host Configuration
bastion_sku = "Standard"

# VM Configuration
vm_sku                 = "Standard_D4as_v5"
vm_name                = "azxpip01"
custom_image_name      = "azxdev01-image-20250314143711"
vm_admin_username      = "hypera_user"
admin_ssh_key_username = "azureuser"
admin_public_key_path  = "~/.ssh/id_ed25519_cafehyna_dev.pub"

# NAT Gateway Configuration
nat_gateway_pip_prefix_length           = 30
nat_gateway_pip_sku                     = "Standard"
nat_gateway_pip_allocation_method       = "Static"
nat_gateway_pip_zones                   = null                      # Assuming no specific zones for the PIP, aligning with NAT GW
nat_gateway_pip_ddos_protection_mode    = "VirtualNetworkInherited" # Default value, can be adjusted if needed
nat_gateway_pip_idle_timeout_in_minutes = 30                        # PIP idle timeout (distinct from NAT GW's 4-min idle timeout)

# Network Configuration
vnet_address_space        = ["10.248.0.0/20"]
vnet_subnet_gateway       = ["10.248.0.0/22"]
vnet_subnet_public        = ["10.248.4.0/22"]
vnet_azure_subnet_bastion = ["10.248.8.0/26"]
vnet_subnet_private       = ["10.248.9.0/24"]
vm_private_ip_address     = ["10.248.9.4"]

# Integration Configuration
msteams_channel              = "https://hypermarcas.webhook.office.com/webhookb2/2a97e7b6-89ee-43b6-9f31-173b2fdc5137@3f7a3df4-f85b-4ca8-98d0-08b1034e6567/IncomingWebhook/7775ad258d6645aa81a34ee0e4e31891/4b4cdf1a-ef4a-4dd8-b404-f6800b484599"
boot_diagnostics_storage_uri = "https://stgdevopsapps.blob.core.windows.net/boot-log"

# Image Reference
custom_image_id = "/subscriptions/e214e4b4-b890-4b78-8e54-6b32b80acc77/resourceGroups/packer_rg/providers/Microsoft.Compute/images/azxdev01-image-20250314143711"

# Security Rules
private_security_rules = [
  {
    name                       = "Allow-Internal"
    priority                   = 100
    direction                  = "Inbound"
    access                     = "Allow"
    protocol                   = "*"
    source_port_range          = "*"
    destination_port_range     = "*"
    source_address_prefix      = "10.248.0.0/20"
    destination_address_prefix = "*"
  },
  {
    name                       = "Allow-HTTPS"
    priority                   = 110
    direction                  = "Inbound"
    access                     = "Allow"
    protocol                   = "Tcp"
    source_port_range          = "*"
    destination_port_range     = "443"
    source_address_prefix      = "10.248.0.0/20"
    destination_address_prefix = "*"
  }
]

public_security_rules = [
  {
    name                       = "Allow-SSH"
    priority                   = 100
    direction                  = "Inbound"
    access                     = "Allow"
    protocol                   = "Tcp"
    source_port_range          = "*"
    destination_port_range     = "22"
    source_address_prefix      = "10.248.8.0/26"
    destination_address_prefix = "*"
  },
  {
    name                       = "Allow-HTTPS"
    priority                   = 110
    direction                  = "Inbound"
    access                     = "Allow"
    protocol                   = "Tcp"
    source_port_range          = "*"
    destination_port_range     = "443"
    source_address_prefix      = "10.248.8.0/26"
    destination_address_prefix = "*"
  }
]

```
════ PAI | NATIVE MODE ═══════════════════════
🗒️ TASK: Dispatch 4 parallel LLM code reviews
```

Dispatching **4 parallel adversarial code review agents** for Story 1-3 — each simulating a different LLM reviewer persona.

input.name: Codex-Reviewer
input.prompt:
You are acting as **OpenAI Codex (O3/GPT-5-Codex)** performing an adversarial code review of Terraform code for Story 1-3: Deploy NAT Gateway and Public IP Resources.

**Your review persona:** Codex — methodical, precision-focused, excels at finding logical inconsistencies, dead code, and variable misuse in infrastructure-as-code.

## Story Context
Story 1-3 deploys a NAT Gateway with static public IP for an Azure Virtual Desktop golden image project. The NAT Gateway uses Azure Verified Modules (AVM) and associates with PrivateSubnet and PublicSubnet.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. NAT Gateway provisioned using AVM module v0.2.1 with Standard SKU, 4-min idle timeout
2. Static Standard public IP and /30 IP prefix assigned
3. NAT Gateway associated with PrivateSubnet and PublicSubnet
4. Public IPs via for_each over local.public_ip_definitions
5. Outputs exposed (NAT GW public IP, prefix CIDR)
6. Tags via local.common_tags

## Files to Review

### terraform/natg.tf
```hcl
module "avm_nat_gateway" {
  source  = "Azure/avm-res-network-natgateway/azurerm"
  version = "0.2.1"

  name                    = local.nat_gateway_name
  location                = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.location
  resource_group_name     = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.name
  idle_timeout_in_minutes = 4
  sku_name                = "Standard"
  tags                    = local.common_tags
  enable_telemetry        = false
  zones                   = null

  public_ips = {
    "packer_image_natg_pip" = {
      name = local.nat_gateway_pip_name
    }
  }

  public_ip_configuration = {
    allocation_method       = var.nat_gateway_pip_allocation_method
    sku                     = var.nat_gateway_pip_sku
    zones                   = var.nat_gateway_pip_zones
    ddos_protection_mode    = var.nat_gateway_pip_ddos_protection_mode
    idle_timeout_in_minutes = var.nat_gateway_pip_idle_timeout_in_minutes
  }
}

resource "azurerm_nat_gateway_public_ip_prefix_association" "natg_prefix" {
  nat_gateway_id      = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id
  public_ip_prefix_id = azurerm_public_ip_prefix.packer-image-natg-ipprefix.id
}
```

### terraform/networking.tf
```hcl
module "virtualnetwork" {
  source  = "Azure/avm-res-network-virtualnetwork/azurerm"
  version = "~> 0.2"

  name                = local.vnet_name
  location            = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.location
  resource_group_name = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.name
  address_space       = var.vnet_address_space
  tags                = local.common_tags

  subnets = {
    GatewaySubnet = {
      name             = "GatewaySubnet"
      address_prefixes = var.vnet_subnet_gateway
    }

    AzureBastionSubnet = {
      name             = "AzureBastionSubnet"
      address_prefixes = var.vnet_azure_subnet_bastion
      network_security_group = {
        id = module.bastion_nsg_avm.resource_id
      }
    }

    PublicSubnet = {
      name             = local.subnet_public_name
      address_prefixes = var.vnet_subnet_public
      network_security_group = {
        id = module.public_nsg_avm.resource_id
      }
      nat_gateway = {
        id = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id
      }
      service_endpoints = [
        "Microsoft.ContainerRegistry",
        "Microsoft.KeyVault",
        "Microsoft.ServiceBus",
        "Microsoft.Storage"
      ]
    }

    PrivateSubnet = {
      name             = local.subnet_private_name
      address_prefixes = var.vnet_subnet_private
      network_security_group = {
        id = module.private_nsg_avm.resource_id
      }
      nat_gateway = {
        id = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### terraform/main.tf
```hcl
resource "azurerm_public_ip_prefix" "packer-image-natg-ipprefix" {
  tags                = local.common_tags
  sku_tier            = "Regional"
  sku                 = "Standard"
  resource_group_name = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.name
  prefix_length       = var.nat_gateway_pip_prefix_length
  name                = local.nat_gateway_pip_prefix_name
  location            = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.location
  ip_version          = "IPv4"
}
```

### terraform/public_ips.tf
```hcl
resource "azurerm_public_ip" "pips" {
  for_each = local.public_ip_definitions

  name                    = each.value.name
  resource_group_name     = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.name
  location                = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.location
  sku_tier                = "Regional"
  sku                     = "Standard"
  allocation_method       = "Static"
  ip_version              = "IPv4"
  idle_timeout_in_minutes = 4
  ddos_protection_mode    = "VirtualNetworkInherited"
  domain_name_label       = each.value.domain_name_label
  tags                    = each.value.apply_common_tags ? local.common_tags : null
}
```

### terraform/outputs.tf (NAT Gateway section)
```hcl
output "nat_gateway_id" {
  description = "The ID of the AVM NAT Gateway."
  value       = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id
}

output "nat_gateway_public_ip_address" {
  description = "The IP address of the Public IP created by the AVM NAT Gateway module."
  value       = module.avm_nat_gateway.public_ip_resource["packer_image_natg_pip"].ip_address
}

output "nat_gateway_public_ip_prefix_value" {
  description = "The CIDR of the Public IP Prefix associated with the NAT Gateway."
  value       = azurerm_public_ip_prefix.packer-image-natg-ipprefix.ip_prefix
}

output "nat_gateway_public_ip_prefix_id" {
  description = "The resource ID of the Public IP Prefix associated with the NAT Gateway."
  value       = azurerm_public_ip_prefix.packer-image-natg-ipprefix.id
}
```

### terraform/locals.tf
